title: hyoscyamus lethalis
intro: Hyoscyamus lethalis, also known as deadly nightshade, is a poisonous plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its shiny black berries and purple flowers. The plant contains tropane alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine for their antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties. Medically, hyoscyamus lethalis has been used to treat conditions such as glaucoma, muscle spasms, and epilepsy, as well as to reduce anxiety and promote sleep. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the leaves and roots, and are often sold in the form of capsules, tinctures, or teas.
